sections.title: File Save & Load (.sqail format)
items: New `.sqail` file format — human-readable JSON envelope with magic identifier, version, kind (sql / diagram / project) and payload

AES-256-GCM encryption for sensitive fields (connection password, API tokens) with two modes: machine key (default, portable between sessions on the same machine) and Argon2id-derived passphrase (cross-machine portable)
Save SQL tabs as plain `.sql` (content as-is, Ctrl+S unchanged) or as `.sqail` (bundles SQL + optional connection with encrypted password + per-tab AI prompt history)
Save diagram tabs as `.sqail` — round-trips full diagram state (tables, annotations, colors, viewport) plus connection and AI history
Open `.sqail` prompts the user before registering a bundled connection — credentials are never silently added to the connection store

sections.title: Projects
items: New "Project" panel in the sidebar (below Schema / History / Saved) — bundle multiple SQL scripts and diagrams plus shared connections into a single `.sqail` project file
Open a project → all files restore as tabs; tabs stay linked to their project file via `projectFileId` so saving the project snapshots the live tab state back into each file
Add active tab to project, rename / remove project files inline

sections.title: Per-Tab AI Prompt History
items: AI command palette recency now lives on `EditorTab` instead of being global — each tab keeps its own prompt/response history that travels with it when saved as `.sqail`
DB-backed global history (Settings → AI History) is unchanged

sections.title: Backend
items: New `crypto` module (AES-256-GCM, Argon2id KDF) and Tauri commands `sqail_encrypt_secret` / `sqail_decrypt_secret`
Per-install machine key stored at `<app_data>/sqail.key` and created on first use

sections.title: Schema Diagram Tab
items: New tab type — open via the tab bar "+ ▾" menu → "New schema diagram" — renders an interactive SVG ER diagram with tables, primary keys, foreign keys, and relationship arrows

Drag-and-drop from the object browser: drop a table onto the canvas and sqail fetches its columns + its schema's foreign keys (cached per schema) and places the table at the drop point; re-dropping an existing table repositions it
Pan by dragging empty space, zoom-to-cursor on mouse wheel (deltaMode-aware, clamped for high-resolution touchpads), Fit to content, Reset view
Per-table header color + global color picker (table header / border / body / PK / FK / relationship / column text) with live updates

Annotations: rectangles, text, and lines — tool buttons auto-reset to "select" after each commit; interactive resize handles (8 for rects, endpoint handles for lines, width + diagonal font-scale for text) that stay constant-sized regardless of zoom
Snap-to-grid toggle (20px) applies to table drags, annotation moves, resize handles, and draft drawing; absolute-position tracking on table drags so snap doesn’t drift
Delete / Backspace removes the selected annotation or hides the selected table from the diagram (respects input focus so typing in inspectors is unaffected)
Exports: PNG (canvas raster), SVG (cloned + viewBox-fitted), PDF (dependency-free — canvas-rastered JPEG embedded in a hand-built PDF), and .drawio (ER-style cells, edges, and annotations)
Table header shows "schema." (dimmed) + "name" (bold) so multi-schema diagrams stay readable

sections.title: Backend
items: New list_foreign_keys Tauri command with dialect-specific SQL for Postgres (information_schema joins), MySQL (key_column_usage), SQLite (per-table pragma_foreign_key_list), and SQL Server (sys.foreign_keys); exposed to the frontend as ForeignKeyInfo

sections.title: Portal
items: Hero callout pills for the diagram feature and local LLM + LoRA training
Features section gains a sixth card ("Visual") for the diagramming feature; "Smart" rewritten to lead with the cloud-or-local choice
AI section adds a "Local LLM & on-device fine-tuning" subsection covering the llama.cpp sidecar, curated Qwen model catalog, and database-tuned LoRA training

sections.title: Database-Tuned LoRA Training
items: New Settings → Model Training tab: pick a configured database connection, a base model from the inline-AI catalog, and kick off a LoRA fine-tune that learns your schema, generated metadata, and sample rows
Dataset builder (Rust) emits JSONL training corpus with schema-level examples, metadata-derived instructions, sample-row WHERE-clause pairs, and join heuristics — dialect-aware quoting and escaping
Python sidecar (scripts/train_sql_lora.py) runs the fine-tune on your local GPU using transformers + peft + trl + datasets; stdout is a JSONL progress stream consumed live by the UI
One-shot environment probe surfaces Python + CUDA + PyTorch + peft versions before you start a job
Live progress: per-job phase, token/step counters, streaming log pane, cancel button; completed adapters show up in the Trained Models catalog
Activate / deactivate an adapter on the running llama.cpp sidecar via --lora — no manual restart dance
Optional GGUF conversion so adapters can be shared across llama.cpp builds

sections.title: Inline AI Model Catalog
items: Expanded catalog: Qwen2.5-Coder-0.5B (CPU-friendly), Qwen2.5-Coder-7B / 14B (performance), Qwen3.5-9B / 27B, Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B (MoE coder-specialist), and Qwen3.6-35B-A3B
All new entries ship with the FIM tokens llama.cpp's /infill endpoint needs for ghost-text — community GGUF mirrors (unsloth / bartowski) where no first-party repo exists
VRAM minimums wired up so the UI can hide models your GPU can't load
Sidecar spawn understands adapter-loaded runs — schema-context retriever and prompt builder keep prompt shape consistent with the fine-tune distribution so quality doesn't regress

sections.title: Auto-update (fix #34)
items: Populate the updater public key in tauri.conf.json so installed apps can verify minisign signatures. The key was empty since v0.4.2, causing 'invalid encoding in minisign data' on Windows updates
CI hard-fails the release when any per-platform .sig file is missing, preventing the release pipeline from silently publishing a manifest with empty signatures
One-time action required: manually install v0.5.4 once — the auto-updater in older installs can't validate the new signature chain

sections.title: Inline AI
items: Fix 'Starting undefined' label during sidecar startup — the Starting variant of the sidecar status was emitted with snake_case model_id while the frontend expected camelCase modelId
Context window dropdown widened to 16384 / 32768 for users routing AI-assistant flows (explain / optimize / docs) through the local sidecar; a sidecar restart is required to apply

sections.title: Inline AI
items: New 'Use as default AI provider' toggle in Settings → Inline AI — when on (and inline AI is enabled + sidecar ready), the AI command palette routes flows with no explicit provider selection through the local sidecar instead of the persisted default

sections.title: Inline AI
items: Sidecar runtime auto-download: first enable fetches the platform-appropriate llama-server (Windows Vulkan, macOS Metal, Linux Vulkan) from the upstream llama.cpp release — no manual install step
New Sidecar runtime row in Settings → Inline AI with status / Download / Delete / cancel
SQAIL_LLAMA_SERVER_PATH still overrides for power users who want CUDA or a custom build
AI assistant palette: new synthetic 'Local (Inline AI)' provider entry appears when inline AI is enabled and the sidecar is ready
Strips wrapping ```sql code fences from SQL-flow responses so local instruct models don't leak markdown into the editor

sections.title: Inline AI completion
items: Ghost-text SQL completion powered by a local llama.cpp sidecar — no cloud calls, nothing leaves your machine
Opt-in: flip the toggle in Settings → Inline AI and pick a model
Three quantised GGUF models in the catalog: Qwen2.5-Coder-1.5B (low-end), Qwen2.5-Coder-3B (default), DeepSeek-Coder-V2-Lite 16B MoE (performance)
Token-budget-aware schema context keeps prompts lean for ~6 ms first-token latency on GPU
Tab to accept, Esc to dismiss; cursor moves and tab/connection switches cancel in-flight requests
Toolbar indicator shows sidecar state at a glance; click to open the Inline AI settings tab
Optional latency telemetry pane for debugging

Bundled llama-server binaries for Windows/macOS are a follow-up; today the feature runs against any OpenAI-compatible local endpoint (Ollama, LM Studio) or a user-supplied llama-server pointed at via SQAIL_LLAMA_SERVER_PATH

sections.title: Auto-Update
items: In-app auto-update: the app checks for new versions on startup and shows a banner when an update is available
One-click download, install, and restart from the update banner
Manual 'Check for Updates' button in Settings > About
Download progress bar with percentage indicator
Tauri updater plugin with signed bundles and GitHub Releases endpoint

sections.title: CI / Build
items: CI pipeline signs update bundles with TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY
New GitHub release job generates latest.json updater manifest and uploads all platform binaries
Helper script for generating updater signing key pair

sections.title: SQL Formatting
items: Stored procedure formatting: embedded SELECT statements now properly formatted with fields on separate lines, aligned AS aliases, and structured layout (previously flattened to a single line)
WHERE/ON clauses now break AND/OR conditions onto separate indented lines
Column alignment capped at 50 chars to avoid excessive whitespace from long expressions
Fixed false table alias prefix on function calls (ISNULL, COUNT, etc.) and string literals
New 'Format with AI' option in the Format dropdown — sends SQL to your AI provider for formatting
AI format diff preview: side-by-side Monaco diff editor with green/red highlighting, Accept/Reject buttons to apply or discard changes
SQL Formatting settings section: configurable indent size, uppercase keywords toggle, AND/OR new line toggle

sections.title: Schema Tree
items: Ctrl+scroll wheel font resizing for the object tree (8–24px range)
Tree font size setting added to Settings > General > Schema Tree
Procedures and Functions now displayed as separate categories in the tree

sections.title: Editor
items: Ctrl+scroll wheel font zoom (changed from Shift to Ctrl for standard convention)
Drag-and-drop support for routines (procedures/functions) from schema tree to editor

sections.title: Settings
items: Cleaner grid-based alignment of settings controls
Routine drop action setting: choose between opening definition or generating exec statement

sections.title: Portal Screenshots Gallery
items: New Screenshots section on the portal with tabbed gallery: Editor, Connections, AI, Split Editor, Light Theme
Editor screenshot embedded in Docs Getting Started panel
Screenshot capture helper script (screenshot-capture.sh) that focuses the sqail window before capture
Screenshot prep script fixed to select the correct Tauri window via _NET_WM_WINDOW_TYPE_NORMAL

sections.title: AI & Editor (from 0.3.4–0.3.6)
items: AI prompt history: last 10 prompts navigable with Up/Down arrows in command palette
AI prompts now include active editor query as context
Split editor can share the same file across panes
DbService connect flow for shared database connections

sections.title: macOS & Build
items: Universal macOS build (arm64 + x86_64) with ad-hoc codesign
macOS download renamed to _universal.dmg
Windows release build fixes
Editor drag-drop fix
Vite bumped to 8.0.5 (security fixes)

sections.title: Portal & Docs
items: Why sqail comparison table vs DBeaver, DataGrip, TablePlus, Beekeeper
macOS Gatekeeper install note with xattr workaround on Downloads page
Brand guide, marketing strategy, and marketing plan documents
README rewritten with five messaging pillars

sections.title: macOS Build Fixes
items: macOS release is now a true Universal binary (arm64 + x86_64) built via --target universal-apple-darwin — runs natively on both Apple Silicon and Intel Macs
Fixed misleading _x64.dmg filename on arm64 hosts: CI output is now sqail_<version>_<build>_universal.dmg
Ad-hoc codesign of the .app bundle after lipo, so every binary inside has a valid signature and will execute on arm64

Workaround documented for 'sqail is damaged and cannot be opened' Gatekeeper error: run xattr -cr /Applications/sqail.app once per install (proper Apple Developer ID signing + notarization still on the roadmap)

sections.title: Portal (sqail.io)
items: New 'Why sqail?' comparison table vs DBeaver, DataGrip, TablePlus, and Beekeeper Studio
macOS install-notes block on the download page with the xattr workaround, highlighted for visitors detected as macOS

sections.title: Docs
items: README rewritten with an elevator pitch, feature bullets keyed to the five messaging pillars, and a macOS install note
New marketing/brand-guide.md documenting the color palette, typography, voice, and messaging pillars

sections.title: AI Assistant
items: Prompt history in the AI command palette — last 10 prompts remembered, navigate with Up/Down arrow keys
AI prompts now include the active editor query as context when relevant

sections.title: Editor
items: Split editor view now mirrors the same file in both panes, allowing you to view different parts of a long query simultaneously
Split panes share a single Monaco model — edits in either pane are instantly reflected in the other

sections.title: DbService Backend
items: JWT authentication added to the DbService HTTP backend
Rust client now calls /api/connections/connect on pool open so subsequent query and metadata calls can reference connections by id
/api/connections/test and /api/connections/connect accept a saved connection id and resolve the connection string from the server's connection store
DbService Windows x64 self-contained release build in the GitHub pipeline, with a download link on the portal

sections.title: Editor
items: Fixed drag-drop of tables from a DbService connection producing 'undefined AS undefined FROM undefined.undefined' — the dbservice driver is now handled by the SELECT generator

sections.title: Dev Tooling
items: scripts/run.sh dev now launches the DbService alongside Tauri and tears it down on exit
scripts/run.sh dev frees ports 5100 (DbService) and 1420 (Vite) before starting to avoid 'address in use' errors

sections.title: Multi-Connection Support
items: Connect to multiple databases simultaneously — each connection maintains its own pool
Tabs are linked to connections: each tab remembers which connection it uses
Switching tabs automatically switches the active connection and reloads the schema tree
Connection color dot shown on each tab to indicate which database it belongs to
New tabs inherit the current active connection
Sidebar shows green dot for all connected databases, not just the active one

sections.title: Saved Queries
items: Loading a saved query opens a new tab (or switches to it if already open)
Duplicate name detection when saving — prompts to overwrite existing query
Tab name syncs with saved query name; custom tab names become the default save name
Saved queries remember the connection they were created with

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Database name dropdown for PostgreSQL and MySQL: fetches available databases when host, port, and user are set
Searchable combobox with type-ahead filtering for database selection

sections.title: UI / UX
items: Splash screen with animated logo, progress bar, and loading status on app startup

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Full native PostgreSQL support: replaced sqlx Any driver with native PgPool, MySqlPool, and SqlitePool for correct handling of all PostgreSQL types (uuid, jsonb, timestamp, numeric, interval, arrays, etc.)
Fixed schema introspection failing on PostgreSQL with 'Any driver does not support the Postgres type PgTypeInfo(Name)' error
Fixed swapped primary key / ordinal position columns in PostgreSQL column listing

sections.title: Editor Tabs
items: Right-click context menu on tabs: Close, Close other tabs, Close tabs to the right, Close all tabs, Rename
Drag a table from the schema tree onto the tab bar to open a new tab with a SELECT statement

sections.title: Query Results
items: Click a row to select it, Ctrl/Cmd+click for multi-select, Shift+click for range select
Selected row count shown in the toolbar
Right-click context menu: Copy (tab-separated), Copy as CSV, Copy as JSON, Copy as Markdown
Keyboard shortcuts: Ctrl+C to copy selected rows, Ctrl+A to select all

sections.title: AI Providers
items: OpenRouter model browser: fetches the full list of available models from openrouter.ai and shows a searchable dropdown in the AI provider settings
Disable SSL certificate verification per AI provider — resolves CRYPT_E_NO_REVOCATION_CHECK errors on corporate networks with TLS inspection
Warning shown in the provider form when SSL verification is disabled

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Windows / Integrated authentication for SQL Server connections via the integrated-auth-gssapi feature flag

sections.title: SQL Editor
items: Database-specific syntax highlighting: PostgreSQL (pgsql), MySQL, SQL Server, SQLite
Context-aware SQL autocomplete: suggests tables after FROM/JOIN, columns after SELECT/WHERE, functions, data types, and schema-qualified identifiers
Alias-aware column completion: typing alias. shows columns from the aliased table (e.g. SELECT t. FROM users t)
Dialect-specific SQL functions in autocomplete: PostgreSQL (STRING_AGG, DATE_TRUNC), MySQL (GROUP_CONCAT, IF), SQL Server (GETDATE, DATEADD)
Stored procedures and functions included in autocomplete suggestions
Autocomplete trigger on dot, comma, and opening parenthesis

sections.title: SQL Validation
items: Typo detection with 'did you mean?' suggestions using Levenshtein distance against SQL keyword dictionary
Dialect-specific error checks: LIMIT in SQL Server, TOP in PostgreSQL, ILIKE outside PostgreSQL, RETURNING in MySQL/MSSQL, backtick quoting in PostgreSQL/MSSQL
Trailing comma detection before clause keywords (FROM, WHERE, GROUP, etc.)
DELETE without FROM clause detection
Improved unclosed string/comment error positioning
Validation re-runs automatically when switching database connections

sections.title: Editor Tabs
items: Rename tabs by double-clicking on the tab title
New tab numbering: always increments from highest existing Query number, avoiding duplicates after closing tabs

sections.title: AI Integration
items: Default AI provider selector dropdown in the AI panel for quick switching
Default provider persisted and used automatically on application start
Metadata generation error messages now displayed in the schema tree sidebar

sections.title: Metadata
items: Click green sparkle button on a table to view existing metadata instead of regenerating
Metadata detail modal accessible directly from the schema tree

sections.title: AI-Powered Metadata Generation
items: Generate metadata documentation for individual tables, schemas, or all objects
Non-streaming AI calls for structured JSON metadata responses
Thinking block stripping for reasoning models
Enhanced JSON parsing with fallback extraction from AI responses
Schema-level metadata generation with progress tracking
Metadata UI with tabs and improved spacing

sections.title: Query History & Saved Queries
items: Query execution history with persistence
Saved queries with create, update, and delete support
Query history and saved queries accessible via commands

sections.title: Editor Enhancements
items: Mouse wheel zoom for editor font size (Shift + scroll)
Window state persistence (size, position) across restarts

sections.title: MSSQL Authentication
items: MSSQL authentication method selector: Entra ID, Windows, and SQL Server auth
Azure Entra ID device code flow with user code display and automatic token polling
Trust Server Certificate option for MSSQL connections
Tenant ID and Azure Client ID fields for Entra ID configuration

sections.title: Build & Platform
items: Linux build scripts (bash) replacing PowerShell-only scripts
Build error fix for tiberius Integrated auth on non-Windows platforms

sections.title: AI Integration
items: AI assistant sidebar panel with 4 flows: Generate SQL, Explain Query, Optimize Query, Generate Documentation
7 AI provider types: Claude, OpenAI, Minimax, Z.ai, Claude Code CLI, LM Studio, OpenAI Compatible
Streaming responses via SSE with real-time output in the sidebar
Schema context injection — automatically includes active database schema in AI prompts
Prompt history with persistence (capped at 100 entries)
AI provider settings: add, edit, delete, set default, test connection
Insert AI-generated SQL directly into the editor
Monaco editor context menu: AI Explain Query and AI Optimize Query

sections.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
items: Global keyboard shortcut system with configurable bindings
Default shortcuts: F5 (Run), Ctrl+S (Save), Ctrl+O (Open), Ctrl+Shift+S (Save As), Ctrl+Shift+N (New Connection), Ctrl+Shift+A (Toggle AI), Ctrl+N (New Tab), Ctrl+W (Close Tab), Ctrl+Shift+F (Format)
Shortcuts persisted in localStorage, survive app restarts

sections.title: File Operations
items: Save query to .sql file (Ctrl+S) with native file dialog
Save As (Ctrl+Shift+S) always prompts for file location
Open .sql file (Ctrl+O) into current or new editor tab
Tab title updates to reflect the saved filename

sections.title: Settings Page
items: Settings modal accessible via toolbar gear icon or Ctrl+,
General tab for future editor/appearance/execution preferences
Keyboard Shortcuts tab with full shortcut configuration
About / Releases tab with version history

sections.title: Project Foundation
items: Tauri v2 desktop app with React + TypeScript + Vite frontend
Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui design system with dark/light theme (system preference)
Collapsible sidebar, resizable editor/results split panel layout

sections.title: Database Connections
items: Support for PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, and SQL Server (MSSQL)
Create, edit, delete, and test database connections
Multiple simultaneous connections with independent pools
Color-coded connection indicators, active state tracking

sections.title: SQL Editor
items: Monaco editor with SQL language mode and custom dark/light themes
SQL keyword autocompletion (90+ keywords, functions, types)
Schema-aware completions: table and column names from connected database
Multi-tab editor with persistence in localStorage
SQL formatting via sql-formatter

sections.title: Query Execution & Results
items: Virtualized data grid (TanStack Table + TanStack Virtual) for large result sets
Sortable columns, row numbers, typed value rendering (NULL, boolean, numbers)
Multiple result sets from semicolon-separated statements
Execution time tracking and error display

sections.title: Schema Browser
items: Tree view: Schema > Tables/Views > Columns with type, PK, nullable info
Double-click or drag-and-drop tables to generate SELECT statements
Right-click context menu: SELECT *, COUNT(*), DESCRIBE, DROP
Auto-refresh on connection change, search/filter
